1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"regexp"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// TestGenID_WorktreePrefix verifies that when NewStore is given an absolute
14	// .ddx path rooted in the real project (e.g. "/path/to/ddx/.ddx"), the
15	// generated bead IDs use the real project directory name as a prefix — even
16	// when the process working directory is an execute-bead linked worktree whose
17	// name would otherwise contaminate the prefix.
18	//
19	// Regression test for: bead-id resolver uses cwd inside worktree
20	// (ddx-7eab13a6).
21	func TestGenID_WorktreePrefix(t *testing.T) {
22		// Build a project directory named "ddx" — the canonical project name.
23		tmpRoot := t.TempDir()
24		projectDir := filepath.Join(tmpRoot, "ddx")
25		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectDir, 0o755))
26	
27		// NewStore receives an absolute path rooted at the real project, not the
28		// worktree.  workingDir inside NewStore will be projectDir.
29		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ddx")
30		s := NewStore(ddxDir)
31		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
32	
33		id, err := s.GenID()
34		require.NoError(t, err)
35	
36		// Must be "ddx-<8 hex digits>", not ".execute-bead-wt-…-<hex>".
37		assert.Regexp(t, regexp.MustCompile(`^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`), id,
38			"bead ID must use real project dir name, not worktree path component")
39	}
40	
41	// TestDetectPrefix_WorktreeDir verifies that detectPrefix, when given an
42	// absolute workingDir that is NOT a linked worktree, returns the base name of
43	// that directory even when the process cwd is a worktree-shaped path.
44	func TestDetectPrefix_WorktreeDir(t *testing.T) {
45		tmpRoot := t.TempDir()
46		projectDir := filepath.Join(tmpRoot, "ddx")
47		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectDir, 0o755))
48	
49		prefix := detectPrefix(projectDir)
50		assert.Equal(t, "ddx", prefix,
51			"detectPrefix should return the project dir name, not the worktree cwd")
52	}
53

commit 8557dd8dded3f7942fcedcffb76abd685f1d248b
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 29 18:02:16 2026 -0400

    fix(bead): normalize worktree cwd before deriving bead ID prefix [ddx-7eab13a6]
    
    detectPrefix() was using an empty workingDir for git commands, causing it
    to run git rev-parse --show-toplevel from the process cwd. When invoked
    from inside an execute-bead linked worktree the cwd is the worktree dir,
    so --show-toplevel returned the worktree path and filepath.Base produced
    a prefix like ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-<...>" instead of "ddx".
    
    Fix: hoist workingDir computation out of the config-load block in NewStore
    and pass it to detectPrefix, which now runs git from the caller-supplied
    project root. The fallback chain is: git --show-toplevel from workingDir →
    filepath.Base(workingDir) → cwd basename → DefaultPrefix.
    
    New tests in worktree_prefix_test.go verify that NewStore with an absolute
    .ddx path rooted at a real project dir ("ddx/") produces bead IDs matching
    ^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$ even when the process cwd is a linked worktree.
    
    Layer-2 design question (in-band bead creation by workers) filed as
    ddx-d3a4f89c. The 22 existing malformed-ID beads are deferred to a
    mass-repair bead pending that design decision (see bead notes).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CGO_ENABLED=1 git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(bead): make worktree-prefix tests fail before the fix [ddx-7eab13a6]

Address review finding: prior tests never chdir'd into a worktree-shaped
path, so they passed on main as well — failing AC #2's "fails-before-fix,
passes-after-fix" requirement.

Both tests now chdir into a worktree-shaped non-git temp dir before
exercising NewStore / detectPrefix. Verified pre-fix code (git show
8557dd8d^) produces ".execute-bead-wt-…-<hex>" and the assertion fires;
post-fix produces "ddx-<8hex>".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. The test now properly demonstrates the bug:

- Pre-fix code: produces `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7eab13a6-...-<hex>` → assertion fails
- Post-fix code: produces `ddx-<8hex>` → assertion passes

This addresses the review finding by making both tests `t.Chdir` into a worktree-shaped non-git directory before exercising `NewStore`/`detectPrefix`, so they fail on main without the fix and pass after it.
